messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip proxy-arp command do?

The ip proxy-arp command enables proxy ARP for the specified Layer 3 interface. Proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces, LAG interfaces, and VLAN interfaces. It is disabled by default. To enable proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no form of this command disables proxy ARP for the specified interface. Syntax: no ip proxy-arp.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag cable-diagnostic command do?

The diag cable-diagnostic command provides information about the cable health after running a diagnostic test on an interface. If you run a new cable diagnostic command when a cable diagnostic is in progress for the interface, the new cable diagnostic command fails to execute. In such a scenario, an error message is displayed. On executing a cable diagnostic test command, it automatically clears the old test results before the new test starts. Public Cable diagnostic commands 194 Specifies the name of the interface. <IF-NAME> test <IF-NAME> Runs a cable diagnostic test on an interface. show <IF-NAME> clear <IF-NAME>. Syntax: diag cable-diagnostic.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the policy command do?

The policy command creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6 class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a policy (use no with the policy command) or an individual policy entry (use no with the sequence number). Specifies the name of the policy. <POLICY-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> Specifies a sequence number for the policy entry. Optional. Ran ge: 1 to 4294967295. comment Stores the remaining entered text as a policy entry comment. class {ip|IPv6} <CLASS- NAME> Specifies a type of class, ip for IPv4, IPv6 for IPv6. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. domain-list The no form of this command removes the domain name. | Parameter | | Description | | | | Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <DOMAIN-NAME> | vrf <VRF-NAME> | | Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> command do?

The ip dns host <HOST-NAME> command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. Public ip dns host 520 <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: no ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip icmp throttle command do?

The ip icmp throttle command used to configure the throttle parameter for both ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 error messages and redirect messages. The no form of this command disables the throttle parameter for both ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 error messages and redirect messages. Specifies the ICMPv4/v6 packet interval in seconds. Default: 1 s econd. Range: 1-86400. <PACKET-INTERVAL>. Syntax: ip icmp throttle <PACKET-INTERVAL>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip icmp unreachable command do?

The ip icmp unreachable command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages on the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. The no form of this command disables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages from the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. This command does not prevent other hosts from sending an ICMP unreachable message. Syntax: no ip icmp unreachable.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ip igmp statistics command do?

The show ip igmp statistics command shows IGMP statistics, including groups joined, or shows statistics by VRF. Specify all-vrfs to show information for all VRFs. all-vrfs Configures drop-unknown and fastlearn modes on the ports. While IGMP snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that made an IGMP request for the multicast. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. This could either be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (vlan-exclusive). The default is vlan-shared. Fast learn enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. Syntax: show ip igmp statistics [all-vrfs].

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command do?

The ip igmp snooping all-vlans command enable IGMP snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables IGMP snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command is issued. When IGMP snooping is enabled globally, IGMP snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global IGMP snooping, allows IGMP snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level IGMP snooping settings. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command do?

The ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command enables the IPv6-only-preferred mode on the VLAN interface, and is also supported on the OOBM interface. Once enabled, the DHCP client prioritizes a IPv6 address over an IPv4 address. To obtain a DHCPv4 address, the IPv6-only-preferred command must be disabled, and the DHCPv4 client must be restarted. This command is disabled by default. NOTE Ensure the server supports the IPv6-only-preferred mode option, as the command will not work unless supported by the server. Public ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred 750 The no form of this command disables IPv6-only-preferred mode. NOTE The IPv6-only-preferred mode command must be configured prior to the ip dhcp command, otherwise it will not take effect. Syntax: no ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command do?

The IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command controls the M fLAGsetting in RAs the router transmits on the current interface. Enable the M flag to indicate that hosts can obtain IP address through DHCPv6. The M flag is disabled by default. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the M flag. 0 0 0 1 Indicates that no information is available via DHCPv6. Indicates that other configuratio n information is available via DH CPv6. Public IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag 769 M flag O flag 1 1 0 1 Indicates that addresses are avail able via Dynamic Host Configura tion Protocol (DHCPv6). If the M flag is set, the O flag is redundant and can be ignored be cause DHCPv6 will return all avai lable configuration information. Syntax: no IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra max-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ra max-interval command configures the maximum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. The interval is calculated to be a value between the current max-interval and min-interval settings. The no form of this command returns the setting to its default, provided the default value is less than the default lifetime value. Specifies the maximum advertisement time in seconds. Range: 4-1800. Default: 600 seconds. <TIME> Configures the minimum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra max-interval <TIME>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094. <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> Specifies the client IPv6 address. Syntax: no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. The no form of this command removes the specified maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives and update it to the default value. Specifies the lifetime of router advertisements sent from this in terface. Range: 4 to 9000 seconds. Syntax: no ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command do?

The aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command enables/disables privilege elevation for the users in an administrators group. When enabled, an administrative user is provided lower privilege access upon successful switch login. The user must execute an enable command and re-authenticate to elevate their privilege to administrator (the administrator level access with ‘#’ prompt). Executing the disable command revokes an administrator privilege and returns the user to the lower privilege access level (the operator level access ‘>’ prompt). The no form of this command disables privilege elevation for the administrator user group. Syntax: no aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST> command do?

Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually. All server groups named in the command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. The no form of this command unconfigures authorization for the specified connection type, reverting to the default of local. NOTE Although only TACACS+ servers are supported for remote authorization, local authorization (basic or full-fledged) can be used with remote RADIUS authentication. Syntax: no aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping command this command configures the drop unknown mode. While MLD snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that initiate an MLD request for multicast. Drop unknown mode can be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (exclusive-vlan). The default configuration is vlan-shared. The no form of this command configures the drop unknown mode on the switch to the default vlan-shared. Required: Enable shared VLAN filter on the switch. vlan-exclusive Required: Enable exclusive drop unknown filter per VLAN. Public MLD snooping global configuration commands 1046 This command disables or reenables MLD snooping on the VLAN. Starting with AOS-CX 10.16, MLD snooping is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables all MLD snooping configurations on the VLAN. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.

The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command enable MLD snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables MLD snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command is issued. When MLD snooping is enabled globally, MLD snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global MLD snooping, allows MLD snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level MLD snooping settings. messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command this command configures the given ports in auto mode, which is the default port mode. The no form of this command disables auto ports. messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command this command enables AOS-CX leaf devices (access-layer switches that connect directly to servers, storage, and other endpoints) to unconditionally forward multicast traffic to all VTEPs that do not support the exchange of MLD reports over the fabric. By default, when MLD snooping is enabled in AOS-CX, multicast traffic is selectively forwarded only to VTEPs connected to have interested hosts. This is achieved by exhanging MLD reports over VXLAN tunnels where each VTEP processes the remote reports and uses the membership information to forward multicast traffic efficiently, conserving fabric bandwidth. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: no crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.

If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command enables or disables MAC authentication globally or at the port-level. Configures the MAC address format that the switch must use in the RADIUS request message. The no form of the command resets the MAC address format to the default, no-delimiter. Configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP • PAP NOTE The PEAP-MSCHAPv2 method of authentication is not supported. The no form of the command resets the authentication method to the default, chap. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Enables and configures the global password that the switch must use for MAC authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for MAC authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Clears the MAC authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator state machines associated to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all MAC authentication enabled ports. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show portfilter command do?

The show portfilter command displays filter settings for all interfaces or a specific interface. <IFNAME> Specifies the ingress interface name. Specifies one of these values: • <FQDN>: a fully qualified domain name. • <IPv4>: an IPv4 address. • <IPv6>: an IPv6 address. Enables or disables port security globally or at the port level. Configures the maximum number of clients that are allowed on a port. After configuring the maximum clients limit, the MAC addresses of the clients can be learned by one of the following methods: • User can manually configure all MAC addresses by using the mac-address command. • User can allow the port to dynamically learn all MAC addresses. • User can configure a fixed number of MAC addresses and allow the switch to learn the remaining addresses dynamically. messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile.. A queue profile maps queues to CoS values. Each profile has two, four, or eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command do?

The ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command enables SSH enforcement that the username must be present in the certificate that is being used for authorization. This configuration alters how certificate-based authentication maps to a user account. When this is enabled, SSH will not require local user association with an authorized-key and instead enforces that the username used to log in is present within the certificate. The SSH server will check for the username in certificate fields Common Name or User Principle Name for a match. If a certificate is not used for authentication then this configuration has no effect on SSH authentication. The no form of this command disables the SSH enforcement of username in the certificate. Syntax: no ssh certificate-as-authorized-key.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vlan port command do?

The show vlan port command displays the VLANs configured for a specific layer 2 interface. Specifies an interface ID. Format: member/slot/port. <INTERFACE-ID> Displays a summary of the VLAN configuration on the switch. Displays the voice VLAN list showing the VLAN ID, name, operational state of the VLAN, and the interfaces associated with the VLAN. Disables a VLAN. (By default, a VLAN is automatically enabled when it is created with the vlan command.) The no form of this command enables a VLAN. Enables VNI mapped VLANs when detecting the presence of a client. When enabled, VNI mapped VLANs are up only if there are authenticated clients on the VLAN, or if the VLAN has statically configured ports and those ports are up. When not enabled, VNI mapped VLANs are always up. Syntax: show vlan port <INTERFACE-ID>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. Public vlan (interface) 2174 If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to. A maximu m of 512 (6000, 6100 Switch Series) VLANs are supported. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface (VSF link) command do?

The interface (VSF link) command enters configuration context for one or more VSF link interfaces. NOTE VSF link interfaces cannot be included in a range with other interfaces. Poet identifier range. Required. <IFRANGE> Creates or modifies a VSF link. The user can specify the physical interfaces that make up the VSF link. Once an interface is part of a VSF link, all existing configuration on the interface is removed and the interface will operate as a VSF interface. At least one interface must be specified for the creation of a VSF link. VSF interfaces carry VSF traffic and can only be connected to other VSF interfaces. Before removing an individual interface from the VSF link using the no vsf link <x> <interface> command, ensure that the interface is administratively shutdown at both local and peer ends. Syntax: interface <IFRANGE>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp vlan command do?

The show arp vlan command displays IPv4 neighbors learnt on L2 VLAN interfaces only when arp-suppression-extended is enabled. Specifies the VLAN ID. Shows information for all VLANs. If global arp-suppression-extended is enabled and per-VLAN arp-suppression-extended is disabled, neighbors learnt on that VLAN are not displayed. If global arp-suppression-extended is disabled and per-VLAN arp-suppression-extended is enabled, neighbors learnt on that VLAN are displayed. Syntax: show arp vlan {VLAN-ID|all}.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Only one VXLAN interface is supported. (Optional) Specify if the interface tunnel uses IPv4 or IPv6 add ressing. If this parameter is not included, the interface default is IPv6. Changes the inter-VXLAN bridging mode on tunnels where VXLAN bridging across tunnels is disabled by default. Default mode is deny. The no form of this command sets the inter-VXLAN bridging mode to the default mode of deny. Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6].

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface vxlan vteps command do?

The show interface vxlan vteps command displays detailed VTEP information for the VXLAN interface. VTEP states can be one of the following: • operational: Tunnel endpoint is fully programmed on the switch hardware. • configuration_error: Tunnel endpoint programming in the switch hardware failed due to misconfiguration. • no_hw_resources: Tunnel endpoint programming failed in the switch hardware due to insufficient resources. • activating: Initial state of tunnel endpoint when it is configured. When a tunnel endpoint is a directly connected via nexthop, then nexthop reachability appears empty (--). Show detail VTEP information. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv6 format (X: X: : X: X). Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IP-ADDR>].

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command do?

The show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command this command displays the status of VLANs with IP fragment bypass enabled. Disables the VXLAN interface and deletes all VXLAN tunnels, segments, and members on the interface. Public shutdown 816 The no form of this command starts the VXLAN interface and creates all VXLAN tunnels and segments. If members are configured, they are added to the VXLAN segment. Configures the source IPv4 address for a VXLAN interface. All VXLAN encapsulated packets use this source IP address in the outer IP header. If you change an existing source IP address, all tunnels with the old source IP address are deleted, and new tunnels are created with the new source IP address. Syntax: show vlan ip-fragment-bypass.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is High Availability?

High Availability: Key goals of HA include: • Achieve five-nines (99.999%) availability of switching traffic through minimization of unplanned network outages. • Fault tolerant: No single active component failure will cause an outage. • Live replacement of hardware with minimal or no disruption.
Terminology: • MM: Abbreviation for management module • MM to MM link: Refers to the 10GbE-KR Ethernet link between two MMs • OVSDB: Abbreviation for Open vSwitch Database.

document_title: High Availability Guide (All Switch Series)
source_file: 6000/10_17_1000/High Availability Guide (All Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is Management Module Failover?

Management Module Failover: There are two types of Management Module (MM) failover: • Controlled failover: The user triggers this type of failover by rebooting the Active MM or running the r edundancy switchover command. • Uncontrolled failover: This type of failover is triggered by unexpected events like a crash on the Active MM or hot removal of the Active MM. In a dual MM chassis, the Standby MM detects failover events in one of the following ways: • A mailbox interrupt is received from the Active MM to indicate takeover. This interrupt can come for controlled or uncontrolled failover (except for a hot removal). • Active MM hot removal detection. • Heartbeat loss detected on the Standby MM for more than 10 seconds. NOTE If the Active MM is not responding and is still not detected by the first two methods, it will be caught by this method.
Failover requirements: • The Standby MM must be present to trigger a failover. An Unassigned MM will never trigger a failover. Public.

document_title: High Availability Guide (All Switch Series)
section: AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules
source_file: 6000/10_17_1000/High Availability Guide (All Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules?

AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules: Consider the following when working with local authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) on switches with multiple management modules: • Local authentication: ◦ The user database is synchronized between the Active and Standby management modules. ◦ Only local users belonging to the administrators group and using local password authentication are permitted to log in to the Standby management module. Alternatively, the Standby management module can be accessed from the Active management module by providing a logged in admin user password. • Local authorization: ◦ A few nonconfiguration commands are available on the Standby management module. ◦ For expert users, the bash shell is available on the Standby management module. • Local accounting: ◦ The audit logs used for local accounting are available only on the Active Management Module.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the bfd echo-src-ip-address command do?

The bfd echo-src-ip-address command sets the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets. This address is used in all echo sessions. NOTE The source IP address must not be on the same network segment as any switch interface, otherwise a large number of ICMP redirect packets may be sent by the remote device, causing network congestion. The no form of this command removes the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets, which causes the switch to stop sending echo packets. When a valid value is set, all sessions with a peer that is capable of receiving echo packets, will start transmitting echo packets. BFD control sessions continue to run concurrently with echo packets. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>. Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>.

If the interval is not set, the default interval is used. NOTE BFD IPv6 Echo is not supported. Specifies the minimum reception interval in milliseconds. A valu e of 0 means that the switch does not support reception of BFD echo packets. Range: 0, 50 to 1000. Default: 500. <INTERVAL>. Syntax: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.

If the interval is not set, the default interval is used. <INTERVAL> Specifies the minimum transmit interval in milliseconds. Range: 500 to 20000 50 to 20000 (8320, 8325, 9300/9300S, 10040). messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about ERPS Commands?

ERPS Commands: Limitations, Conflicts, or Exclusions • ERPS coexists with STP with the following limitations: Public Limitations, Conflicts, or Exclusions 82 ◦ ERPS control VLANs shall not be part of RPVST. ◦ Ring ports are excluded from STP operation. NOTE It is not recommended to configure any Spanning Tree interface context- related commands on the ERPS ring port. Before configuring ring port ensure all Spanning Tree interface context-related commands are removed from the interface. ◦ With VSX, STP operates on ISL ports despite being ring ports. ◦ Only default MSTP instance (CIST) is supported with ERPS. ◦ ERPS cannot be enabled with more than 252 RPVST instances. This limitation is applicable for all supported platforms. • Dynamic VLANs (MVRP) are not supported on ERPS ring ports. • MCLAG configuration on ERPS ring ports is not supported. • ERPS and loop protect are not supported on the same port. If enabled together, the behavior is undefined. • Active GW is not recommended on VLANs that are not part of VSX-LAGs.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erps ring <RINGID> guard-interval 93 command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> guard-interval 93 command guard timer is used in nodes recovering from a local failure to avoid loops due to earlier Signal Fail (SF) messages that may be in the ring. The configuration specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. The timer period must be greater than the maximum expected forwarding delay in which an R-APS message traverses the entire ring. The default value is 50. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the guard interval and sets it to the default value of 50. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 Required, specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. Default: 50. <RINGID> <10 milliseconds>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi-instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> role 110 command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> role 110 command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching on/off the RPL link. This command specifies the role of the node as owner or neighbor. The no form of this command removes the configuration of the node role from the instance. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> <RPL-OWNER> <RPL-NEIGHBOR> Required, specifies the ERPS ring instance identifier. Range: 1 -2. Blocks traffic at one end of the RPL. The blocked end sends out periodic R-APS. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

A persona LAG defines configuration options that can be applied to multiple LAGs for consistency and simplified management. Only LAG interfaces can reference persona LAG templates. The persona LAG name cannot use reserved interface names or formats, such as vlan, lag, or loopback. Configurations defined in a persona LAG are valid only for LAG interfaces. messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip address command do?

The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading zeros. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192.168.5.1 00. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. Specifies a secondary IP address. <IPv4-ADDR> <MASK> secondary. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server rest access-mode command do?

The https-server rest access-mode command changes the REST API access mode. The default mode is read-write. This command does not affect Central connections, which have permission to alter configurations regardless of the access mode set on the switch. Selects the read/write mode. Allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DE LETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the s witch database. Selects the read-only mode. Write access to most switch resou rces through the REST API is disabled. Setting the mode to read-write on the REST API allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the switch database. By default, REST APIs in the device are in the read-write mode. Some switch resources allow POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE regardless of REST API mode. Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.

A successful response contains a JSON format with the count key and a number as the value. It works with requests including wildcard and other query parameters. Omitting it in the request is equivalent to specifying count=false. Including it in the request as count= is equivalent to specifying count=true. messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about CLI Management Interface?

CLI Management Interface: Introduction Firmware Site Distribution (FWSD) is an efficient feature designed to allow the client to upgrade multiple devices in a network simultaneously. The orchestrator coordinates the entire upgrade process, providing better control and monitoring. The feature can handle a large number of devices, making it suitable for networks of various sizes. Devices in the network can retrieve firmware images from seed devices, minimizing the load on external sources. This feature can be used by a CLI command or a REST request. It's possible to execute the firmware site distribution via CLI and the REST management interface request at the same time. The switch can handle up to 2 concurrent requests if the requests share the same SSL session. Although it is possible, it is recommended to use only one to avoid any inconvenience. FWSD is particularly suitable for environments with a large number of devices which are locally co-located. FWSD operates as follows: 1. The orchestrator determines which devices require upgrades. 2. The orchestrator sends upgrade commands to the pending devices. 3.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed} command do?

The mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed} command configures a Layer 2 or VXLAN interface to generate SNMP trap notifications for up to four different types of MAC address related events on the trunk or access in physical or lag interfaces. MAC notification trap addition to or removal from an interface can be in any combination, quantity, or order. The addition of existing configured traps or removal of non-configured traps will be accepted and ignored. NOTE The mac-notify feature must be enabled globally for any interface configurations to generate SNMP traps. Enabling mac-notify traps may impact the system performance on networks with a large number of mac-notify events. The no form of this command removes the traps from the interface. Notifies when a MAC address aged out on the interface. Syntax: mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed}.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command do?

The rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command enables and disables the RMON alarm and its index. RMON alarm is enabled by default. Enables the RMON alarm index Disables the RMON alarm index. index <INDEX> Specifies the RMON alarm index. Range: 1 to 20. all. Syntax: rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all}.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server agent-port command do?

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. Public snmp-server agent-port 52 The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Specifies the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent wil l use. Range: 1 to 65535. Default: 161. <PORT>. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. 4100i, 6000, and 6100 only support default VRF. The SNMP agent can listen on multiple VRFs. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Specifies the name of a VRF. <VRF-NAME> Public snmp-server vrf 69. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 user <NAME> command do?

The snmpv3 user <NAME> command creates an SNMP v3 user and adds it to an SNMP v3 context. The SNMP v3 security level (set with command snmpv3 security-level) determines which users are allowed to authenticate. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v3 user. NOTE When updating the authentication protocols and privacy protocols for the existing SNMP v3 users, you must also update the access level. Otherwise, the access level will be set to read-only. <NAME> access-level auth <AUTH-PROTO> Specifies the SNMP v3 username. Range 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question mark (?). Syntax: snmpv3 user <NAME>.
